# StageSet Controller

> ordered, gated, multi-stage delivery of Flux sources (Git, OCI, Bucket, ExternalArtifact)

Project home: https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/ — full text for LLMs: https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/llms-full.txt

## Get started

- [From Jsonnet to a gated rollout](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/get-started/jsonnet-to-rollout/): Write Jsonnet manifests, render them with JaaS, and roll them out with StageSet — end to end.
- [Install on Kubernetes](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/get-started/kubernetes/): Prerequisites, the idempotent Helm deploy, how upgrades treat the CRDs, where to customize, and how to verify the controller is healthy.
- [Quickstart](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/get-started/quickstart/): Install the controller and roll out your first StageSet in a few steps.
- [Stage sources — Git, OCI, Bucket](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/get-started/flux-sources/): Point a stage straight at a Git/OCI/Bucket source, or render manifests first into an ExternalArtifact.

## Guides

- [Best practices](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/guides/best-practices/): How to get the most out of StageSet — keep the render pure, model the release as gated stages, choose action scopes deliberately — and the anti-patterns that bite.
- [Generating migrations with Jsonnet](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/guides/jsonnet-migrations/): Render a version-gated migration ladder with JaaS and consume it from a StageSet via migrationsSourceRef.
- [Parameterizing a rollout](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/guides/parameters/): Two layers of parameters — JaaS TLAs/extVars at render time, StageSet postBuild substitution at delivery.
- [Progressive delivery](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/guides/progressive-delivery/): Gate a Flagger or Argo Rollouts promotion on a StageSet stage, and gate a StageSet stage on a Rollout.

## Defining a release

- [Actions](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/defining-a-release/actions/): Typed pre, post, and on-failure steps — jobs, HTTP gates, waits, patches, deletes, transient applies.
- [Conflict policies](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/defining-a-release/conflict-policies/): Resolve immutable-field and ownership conflicts per resource.
- [Inventory and pruning](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/defining-a-release/inventory/): How a StageSet records what each stage applied — the sharded StageInventory — prunes what a revision no longer renders, and labels every applied object for per-stage discovery.
- [Ready checks](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/defining-a-release/ready-checks/): Gate a stage on health with kstatus and CEL expressions.
- [Stages and sources](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/defining-a-release/stages-and-sources/): The core — ordered stages applying Flux sources, with path, prune, patches, and substitution.

## Gating & rollout safety

- [Error-budget freeze](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/gating/error-budget/): Freeze new-revision rollouts while a service is out of its SLO error budget, resuming on its own when it recovers.
- [Fleet rollout](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/gating/fleet-rollout/): Roll one version across a fleet of StageSets in ordered waves — canary a few tenants, soak, health-check, then widen, and halt the whole fleet on regression.
- [Rollback](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/gating/rollback/): Restore the last good artifact revision when a run fails.
- [Stage promotion](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/gating/stage-promotion/): Hold a rollout at a stage with a soak window or a manual gate before it advances.
- [Update windows](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/gating/update-windows/): Allow or deny rollouts on a schedule, without pausing reconciliation.
- [Versioned migrations](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/gating/versioned-migrations/): Run migrations once, when the deployed version crosses a release boundary — inline, or sourced from a Flux artifact and shared across StageSets.

## Security & multi-tenancy

- [Admission webhook](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/security/admission-webhook/): The validating webhook for StageSet — the invariants it enforces, why it exists instead of CEL, the failure-policy trade-off, and the two TLS provisioning modes.
- [Image verification](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/security/image-verification/): Gate a rollout on container image signatures — cluster ImageVerificationPolicies verify each image with cosign keyless identity and pin it to its digest before a stage applies it.
- [Multi-cluster and tenancy](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/security/multi-cluster/): Impersonation, watch scoping, single-tenant cluster-admin, and remote clusters.
- [Network policy](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/security/network-policy/): The opt-in NetworkPolicy the chart ships — pod-scoped allowlists vs. a namespace-wide default-deny, choosing a policy engine, the ingress and egress traffic the controller needs, and how to tighten each port.
- [Secrets encryption](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/security/encryption/): Decrypt SOPS-encrypted files in a stage's source before they are applied.
- [Service mesh](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/security/service-mesh/): The opt-in service-mesh authorization the chart ships — Istio or Linkerd identity-based authorization and mTLS layered over networkPolicy, per-port allowed mesh identities, the non-mesh carve-outs for the apiserver and kubelet, and native passthrough.

## Integrations & tooling

- [Claude Code skill](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/integrations/claude-code/): Install the StageSet plugin so Claude Code authors and operates StageSets.
- [Flux Operator (ResourceSet) integration](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/integrations/flux-operator/): Feed discovered versions into gated promotion, or fan out one StageSet per input.
- [MCP server](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/integrations/mcp-server/): Run the stageset-controller Model Context Protocol server so an agent reads StageSet status and drives reconciliation in-cluster.
- [Producer-aware sources](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/integrations/producer-aware-sources/): Reference a producer like JaaS and let the controller find its ExternalArtifact.

## Run in production

- [Backup and disaster recovery](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/running/disaster-recovery/): Back up the state the controller cannot fully reconstruct, and restore a cluster in the order that keeps pruning, teardown, and decryption safe.
- [Operations](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/running/operations/): Metrics, alerts, events, and runbooks for running the controller day to day.
- [Production](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/running/production/): A decision-oriented checklist for hardening a stageset-controller install before it manages production releases.
- [Scale and capacity](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/running/scale-and-capacity/): Size a controller replica for your workload, decide how many replicas to run, tune the throughput knobs, and read the metrics that tell you when you are at capacity.

## Observability

- [Alerting](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/observability/alerting/): The controller's Kubernetes Events on StageSet transitions, the opt-in PrometheusRule alert catalog with tunable thresholds, and how every alert and Ready reason links to a runbook.
- [Dashboard](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/observability/dashboard/): The StageSet controller ships a maintained Grafana dashboard as a single-layer OCI image — render it through JaaS and hand it to the grafana-operator.
- [Logging](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/observability/logging/): The controller's structured slog output, the --log-level and --log-format flags, reading logs with kubectl and jq, and the chart values that drive them.
- [Metrics](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/observability/metrics/): The controller's Prometheus metrics — the stageset_* custom series and controller-runtime signals, scraping with a ServiceMonitor or a plain config, querying with PromQL, and the chart values that drive them.
- [Service level objectives](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/observability/slos/): The StageSet controller's SLOs — reconcile availability and reconcile latency — with their SLIs, targets, error budget, the dashboard that shows them, and how to tune them.
- [Tracing](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/observability/tracing/): OpenTelemetry tracing for the controller — the --tracing-endpoint, --tracing-insecure, and --tracing-sample-ratio flags, pointing at an OTLP collector, and the chart values that drive them.

## CLI

- [stagesetctl apply](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/cli/apply/): Server-side-apply a StageSet's rendered manifests to the cluster, in stage order, under the controller's field manager.
- [stagesetctl baseline](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/cli/baseline/): Assert a once-per-lifetime action already completed (adoption), or export a ledger's completions as a committable baseline.
- [stagesetctl build](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/cli/build/): Render a StageSet's manifests to stdout, exactly as the controller would.
- [stagesetctl diff](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/cli/diff/): Preview what a StageSet would change in the cluster — usable as a CI gate.
- [stagesetctl fleet](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/cli/fleet/): Show a FleetRollout's wave-by-wave progress — which members are at the target, held, or regressed.
- [stagesetctl get](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/cli/get/): Print human-readable StageSet status, or list StageSets.
- [stagesetctl lint-migrations](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/cli/lint-migrations/): Validate a migration ladder file or directory before publishing it to a source.
- [stagesetctl plan](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/cli/plan/): Preview what the next reconcile will do — which actions run, skip, or re-run, and why.
- [stagesetctl reconcile](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/cli/reconcile/): Force an out-of-band reconcile, optionally waiting for it to be handled.
- [stagesetctl reset-ledger](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/cli/reset-ledger/): Forget a StageLedger completion so its once-per-lifetime action runs again.

## API reference

- [StageInventory](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/api/stageinventory/): The controller-managed inventory of objects each stage applied.
- [StageSet](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/api/stageset/): Every field of the StageSet resource — the one you author.

## Reference

- [Configuration reference](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/reference/configuration/): Every stageset-controller command-line flag and its default.
- [Helm chart values](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/reference/helm-values/): Complete reference for every value the stageset-controller Helm chart exposes, generated from the chart's values.schema.json.
- [Upgrading](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/reference/upgrading/): The actions required when upgrading stageset-controller, one section per release that needs migration steps.
- [Verifying releases](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/reference/verifying-releases/): Verify the signed container image, release archives, and stagesetctl CLI before you run them.

## Comparisons

- [StageSet vs Argo Rollouts](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/comparisons/argo-rollouts/): Different layers — staged multi-artifact delivery versus single-workload traffic shifting.
- [StageSet vs Flux kustomize-controller](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/comparisons/flux/): Intra-release stages and gates versus per-Kustomization ordering.
- [StageSet vs Flux Operator ResourceSet](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/comparisons/flux-operator/): Horizontal fan-out and scheduled discovery versus vertical, gated promotion.
- [StageSet vs Helm](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/comparisons/helm/): Templating and install/upgrade hooks versus ordered, gated delivery with a durable action lifecycle.
- [StageSet vs jsonnet-controller](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/comparisons/jsonnet-controller/): A jsonnet-native Flux applier versus a renderer-agnostic staged-delivery layer.
- [StageSet vs Kustomize](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/comparisons/kustomize/): Why StageSet delivers what Kustomize only builds.
- [StageSet vs Tanka and kubecfg](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/comparisons/tanka-kubecfg/): Reconciled, gated delivery versus CLI-driven Jsonnet apply.

## Runbooks

- [ActionDirty](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/actiondirty/): A stage with an incomplete once-per-lifetime action failed repeatedly, so the controller halted auto-retry; clear the halt with a manual reconcile once the cause is fixed.
- [ArtifactNotFound](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/artifactnotfound/): The referenced ExternalArtifact could not be found (transient; the controller requeues).
- [AwaitingApproval](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/awaitingapproval/): A version transition is held for approval; approve the target version to proceed.
- [AwaitingPromotion](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/awaitingpromotion/): A stage is applied and healthy and the rollout is holding for a manual promotion.
- [BudgetExhausted](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/budgetexhausted/): A rollout is frozen because the service is out of its SLO error budget.
- [BudgetSourceUnavailable](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/budgetsourceunavailable/): A metric source for an error-budget freeze or a promotion analysis could not be read.
- [Controller pod down](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/controller-pod-down/): A stageset-controller pod has been NotReady for the alert window.
- [DependencyNotReady](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/dependencynotready/): A StageSet named in spec.dependsOn is not yet Ready.
- [DowngradeNotAllowed](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/downgradenotallowed/): The desired version is below the deployed version and downgrades are not enabled.
- [DowngradeRequiresMigration](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/downgraderequiresmigration/): A downgrade is enabled, but a version boundary it crosses declares no down actions and is irreversible.
- [ImageUnverified](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/imageunverified/): A stage referenced an image that fails an ImageVerificationPolicy; the stage is held before apply.
- [InvalidSpec](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/invalidspec/): The StageSet spec is invalid; the Message names the offending field or action.
- [InvalidVersion](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/invalidversion/): A version source or value could not be parsed as semver.
- [MigrationArtifactInvalid](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/migrationartifactinvalid/): A migration ladder sourced via spec.migrationsSourceRef could not be parsed or failed content validation; the version is held back until the artifact is fixed.
- [MigrationCoverageMissing](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/migrationcoveragemissing/): A major-version transition has no migration covering it, and requireMigrationCoverage is set; the version is held back.
- [MigrationDirty](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/migrationdirty/): A migration failed repeatedly, so the controller halted auto-retry; clear the halt with a manual reconcile once the cause is fixed.
- [MigrationFailed](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/migrationfailed/): A migration's action failed; the run halts at that stage and retries with backoff, skipping already-completed actions.
- [MigrationSourceNotPinned](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/migrationsourcenotpinned/): A sourced migration ladder's source is pinned to a mutable tag/branch instead of an immutable digest/commit, and pinning is required.
- [MigrationSourceNotVerified](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/migrationsourcenotverified/): A sourced migration ladder's source is not signature-verified; the destructive ladder is refused until verification passes.
- [MigrationStageNotFound](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/migrationstagenotfound/): A version-selected migration anchors to a stage that doesn't exist in this StageSet; the version is held back rather than skipping the migration.
- [PreviousRevisionUnavailable](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/previousrevisionunavailable/): rollbackOnFailure is set but the last-good revisions could not be restored.
- [Producer watch engagement failing](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/watch-engagement/): A dynamic watch on a producer source kind failed to engage, so StageSets referencing that kind no longer re-trigger on the producer's upstream changes.
- [PromotionBlocked](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/promotionblocked/): A stage's promotion analysis breached its thresholds, so the rollout will not advance past it.
- [RBACDenied](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/rbacdenied/): An apiserver call the controller made — resolving a source, an impersonated apply, or a tenant get — failed with Forbidden or referenced a kind the apiserver does not know.
- [Reconcile latency high](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/reconcile-latency/): Reconcile p99 latency for the StageSet controller is above threshold.
- [ResolveFailed](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/resolvefailed/): A source reference could not be resolved to a ready ExternalArtifact.
- [Soaking](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/soaking/): A stage is applied and healthy and the rollout is holding through its promotion soak window before advancing.
- [SourceNotReady](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/sourcenotready/): The source exists but has not published a ready artifact yet (transient).
- [StageFailed](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/stagefailed/): A stage failed to fetch, build, apply, verify, or run an action; the run halts there.
- [Stalled](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/stalled/): The run cannot make progress and will not retry until the spec changes.
- [Succeeded](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/succeeded/): All stages applied and verified the healthy steady state.
- [Suspended](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/suspended/): Reconciliation is paused via spec.suspend.
- [TeardownForced](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/teardown-forced/): A deleting StageSet's finalizer was force-dropped after teardown kept failing past --max-teardown-wait, possibly orphaning objects on the target cluster.
- [UpdateDeferred](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/updatedeferred/): A new revision is held by a closed update window.
- [Webhook cert renewal failing](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/webhook-cert-renewal/): The self-signed admission webhook certificate is not being rotated.
- [Workqueue saturation](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/runbooks/workqueue-saturation/): The controller cannot drain its reconcile queue fast enough.

## Contributing

- [Building and testing](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/contributing/building/): Build the controller, run the test suite, and pass the static-analysis gate.
- [CI and releases](https://stageset.projects.metio.wtf/contributing/ci-and-release/): The pull-request gates and the automated calendar-based release process.
